summary: Multiple mainstream AI agent releases (OpenHands 1.11.0, Aider v0.86.2/v0.85.0, pydantic-ai v2.8.0, Langfuse v3.210.0) alongside new platform moves (Mistral Vibe for code, Daytona $24M Series A for sandboxes, Supabase/OpenCode integration) and emerging infra (nono sandbox, Kilo platform). Key gaps in official changelogs from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, Cursor, and in user field reports from social/discussion platforms. | OpenHands cloud releases, Grok 4.5 pricing, and user discussions on coding agent tooling and runtime validation dominate the discourse.

gaps: Missing user field reports from social/discussion (Bluesky, Reddit, HN, X) — need operator experiences with coding agents
Missing user_workflow signals: no concrete operator scenarios reported in source set
Missing mainstream_product: Apple, Meta, xAI/Grok, Vercel, Cloudflare, E2B, Replit, Devin/Windsurf, Warp, DeepSeek, Qwen, OpenHands already covered; no evidence from others
Missing user_workflow: concrete operator field reports beyond tooling surveys.
Missing infra_primitive: only 2 candidates (Zedra, Cordium) – need more sandbox/runtime/eval signals.
